description:
You are a Site Reliability Engineer who has just received a JSON file that contains the results of several uptime checks for internal micro-services. Your objectives are: 1. Validate that the JSON data file conforms to the provided JSON Schema. 2. Produce a one-file statistical summary of the checks using jq. Files and directories already present in the system: • /home/user/data/uptime_checks.json – the raw check data (an array of objects). • /home/user/data/uptime_schema.json – a Draft-07 JSON Schema describing the shape of each check object. • /home/user/output/ – an empty writable directory where all output must be written. Required results: A. Validation • Use any CLI JSON-schema validator available in the container (for example, jsonschema, ajv, check-jsonschema, etc.). • If validation succeeds, create the text file /home/user/output/schema_validation.log containing exactly one line: 
VALID
• If validation fails, the file must contain exactly one line: 
INVALID
B. Statistical summary • Using only jq, calculate: – total_checks: total number of elements in the array. – failed_checks: number of elements whose status property is not the string "OK". – average_response_time_ms: the mean of the response_time_ms property across all elements, rounded to the nearest integer. • Create the file /home/user/output/uptime_summary.json whose entire contents must be a single JSON object with the exact three keys above and values as numbers. • The JSON object must be written on a single line with no trailing newline. Example format (values will differ): 
{"total_checks":5,"failed_checks":2,"average_response_time_ms":187}
Success criteria checked by the automated grader: 1. The file /home/user/output/schema_validation.log exists and contains precisely either VALID or INVALID. 2. A file /home/user/output/uptime_summary.json exists, is valid JSON, and the three numeric values match those calculated from /home/user/data/uptime_checks.json. 3. No additional files are created inside /home/user/output/. Perform whatever terminal commands you need (validation + one jq pipeline for the statistics is enough). When you are done, the grading script will read the two files to determine success.
truth:
Initial files provided to the agent:

1. /home/user/data/uptime_checks.json  
Contents (single line, UTF-8, no trailing newline):  
[{"service_name":"auth","status":"OK","response_time_ms":120},{"service_name":"billing","status":"FAIL","response_time_ms":310},{"service_name":"search","status":"OK","response_time_ms":95},{"service_name":"cart","status":"OK","response_time_ms":210},{"service_name":"email","status":"FAIL","response_time_ms":275}]

2. /home/user/data/uptime_schema.json  
Contents (pretty-printed, but parser should ignore whitespace):  
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "array",
  "items": {
    "type": "object",
    "required": ["service_name", "status", "response_time_ms"],
    "properties": {
      "service_name": { "type": "string" },
      "status": { "type": "string", "enum": ["OK", "FAIL"] },
      "response_time_ms": { "type": "number", "minimum": 0 }
    },
    "additionalProperties": false
  }
}

Expected facts the grader will check:

• The validation must succeed (the JSON conforms), so the correct content for /home/user/output/schema_validation.log is exactly:  
VALID

• Computations:  
  total_checks = 5  
  failed_checks = 2 (billing, email)  
  average_response_time_ms = round((120+310+95+210+275)/5)  
                            = round(1010/5)  
                            = round(202)  
                            = 202  

Therefore /home/user/output/uptime_summary.json must contain exactly (no newline):  
{"total_checks":5,"failed_checks":2,"average_response_time_ms":202}

The grader will fail the task if:
• Either output file is missing, has extra whitespace/newlines, or contains wrong values.
• Additional unexpected files are found in /home/user/output/.
